page: Microsoft Azure M832is_16_v3 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the MISHighMemoryv3 server family. It is powered by x86_64 Intel processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 832 dedicated vCPUs and a massive memory capacity of 15,200.0 GB, which translates to approximately 18.27 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include any local storage, relying instead on its Premium Storage capabilities for remote disk attachments, and it does not feature any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Operating in an isolated environment, the M832is_16_v3 provides dedicated compute resources, ensuring that workloads are shielded from multi-tenant interference. The primary tradeoff of this configuration is the complete absence of local storage and hardware acceleration, meaning all storage operations must go over the network to Premium Storage, and parallel processing tasks cannot leverage GPUs. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents an extremely high-density hardware class designed for massive scale. The massive memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it a specialized resource where the cost is justified by the sheer volume of RAM and compute cores consolidated into a single isolated instance.

The M832is_16_v3 is engineered for large-scale, memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is highly suitable for massive in-memory databases, large-scale SAP HANA deployments, heavy data warehousing, and complex enterprise resource planning systems. Additionally, the 832 dedicated vCPUs make it capable of handling large-scale data processing, analytics, and memory-bound scientific simulations that require massive parallel processing within a single system image.
